name: Dolmen de Magacela — circular por la Laguna de los Santos
region_intro: Hiking around Magacela offers routes through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, agricultural plains, and historical sites. The region features varied terrain, including paths near the Guadalefra River and around the Laguna de los Santos. Elevations are generally moderate, with some routes passing by ancient dolmens and the Magacela Castle. Best hiking trails around Magacela The most popular hiking route is Camino de Piedraescrita al Río Guadalefra — round trip from Campanario, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route features river views and open countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Dolmen de Magacela — circular por la Laguna de los Santos, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This circular route explores historical sites and natural wetlands. Local hikers also love the Magacela Castle and Mozarabic Way Loop, a 10.2 miles (16.5 km) trail leading through historical landmarks and traditional paths, often completed in about 4 hours 29 minutes. Hiking around Magacela is defined by historical trails, riverine landscapes, and open agricultural areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging long distance hikes Magacela. The routes in Magacela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from 5 reviews. More than 70 hikers have used komoot to explore Magacela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Magacela?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes around Magacela, offering a diverse range of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging long-distance treks. The region features varied terrain, including paths near the Guadalefra River and around the Laguna de los Santos.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Magacela?
A: Yes, Magacela offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293570180/dolmen-de-magacela-circular-por-la-laguna-de-los-santos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela — circular por la Laguna de los Santos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.6-mile (4.2 km) path that explores historical sites and natural wetlands.

Q: What kind of historical sites can I see while hiking in Magacela?
A: Magacela is rich in history. Many trails pass by ancient landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292088322/magacela-castle-and-mozarabic-way-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle and Mozarabic Way Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to explore these historical sites.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Magacela?
A: Yes, Magacela features several circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293570180/dolmen-de-magacela-circular-por-la-laguna-de-los-santos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela — circular por la Laguna de los Santos&lt;/a&gt;, which offers an easy loop through historical and natural landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292088322/magacela-castle-and-mozarabic-way-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle and Mozarabic Way Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the longer distance hiking options around Magacela?
A: For those seeking longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293563135/mozarabic-way-magacela-and-la-haba-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mozarabic Way: Magacela and La Haba Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer a challenging experience, covering over 15 miles (24 km). These trails often lead through traditional paths and open agricultural areas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Magacela?
A: The hiking routes in Magacela are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.5 stars from 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the historical trails, riverine landscapes, and the peaceful open agricultural areas that define the region's charm.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt; itself provides panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2458178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabra Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed for enjoying the expansive landscape.

Q: Can I find trails that follow rivers or pass by water features?
A: Absolutely. Several routes in Magacela feature river views, particularly those near the Guadalefra River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293588024/camino-de-piedraescrita-al-rio-guadalefra-round-trip-from-campanario" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Piedraescrita al Río Guadalefra — round trip from Campanario&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail known for its riverine landscapes and open countryside.

Q: What is the terrain like for hiking in Magacela?
A: The terrain around Magacela is characterized by rolling hills, agricultural plains, and historical sites. Elevations are generally moderate, making it accessible for various fitness levels. You'll encounter a mix of paths near rivers, through wetlands, and across open fields.

Q: Are there any other towns or attractions nearby worth exploring after a hike?
A: Yes, after your hike, you might consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6080107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt;, a nearby settlement that offers additional points of interest. The region also features the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Pared&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Magacela?
A: While specific seasonal details are not provided, regions with agricultural plains and moderate elevations like Magacela are generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter might offer cooler, crisp conditions.
tips: On the north side of the town of Magacela we find this prehistoric building that is in perfect condition. From one side of the Dolmen you can see the castle of the same town in the background.
